summary: DHH shares a scanned copy of a Danish newspaper article from Jyllands-Posten that featured him and two other bloggers discussing the blogging phenomenon. The brief post is a meta-commentary on early blogging culture, with DHH quoting himself on the addictive nature of self-expression online. He playfully acknowledges the dubious legality of scanning and sharing the newspaper article.
key_points: DHH was featured in a mainstream Danish newspaper article about the emerging blogging phenomenon in 2002
He frames blogging as an addictive form of self-expression that becomes hard to abandon
The post reflects the early-2000s tension between traditional media and the nascent blogosphere
money_quotes: Once you've stepped on board the carousel of self-expression, you're not getting off voluntarily
Let's hope they don't notice and leave the sabre of cease and desist sheathed
key_insight: Even in 2002, DHH recognized that personal publishing online was a one-way door—once people started expressing themselves publicly, the habit would prove irresistible.
